revision.text:
'''金山彦神'''（かなやまひこのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。[[古事記]]では'''金山毘古神'''、[[日本書紀]]では'''金山彦神'''と表記する。'''金山毘売神'''（金山姫神）とともに[[鉱山]]の神として信仰されている。
[[神産み]]において、[[イザナミ]]が火の神[[カグツチ]]を産んで火傷をし病み苦しんでいるときに、その嘔吐物（たぐり）から化生した神である。古事記では金山毘古神・金山毘売神の二神、日本書紀の第三の一書では金山彦神のみが化生している。
神名の通り「金山」（かなやま、鉱山）を司る神で、嘔吐物から産まれたとしたのは、嘔吐物の外観からの連想によるものと考えられる。鉱山を司どり、また荒金を採る神とされ、[[鉱業]]・[[鍛冶]]など、[[金属]]に関する技工を守護する神とされている。[[岐阜県]][[垂井町]]の[[南宮大社]]（金山彦神のみ）、[[宮城県]][[牡鹿町]]の[[黄金山神社]]を始め、全国の'''金山神社'''で祀られている。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の神の一覧]] *[[アマツマラ]] *[[イシコリドメ]]
[[Category:日本の神|かなやまひこ]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''金山彦神'''（かなやまひこのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。[[古事記]]では'''金山毘古神'''、[[日本書紀]]では'''金山彦神'''と表記する。'''金山毘売神'''（金山姫神）とともに[[鉱山]]の神として信仰されている。
[[神産み]]において、[[イザナミ]]が火の神[[カグツチ]]を産んで火傷をし病み苦しんでいるときに、その嘔吐物（たぐり）から化生した神である。古事記では金山毘古神・金山毘売神の二神、日本書紀の第三の一書では金山彦神のみが化生している。
神名の通り「金山」（かなやま、鉱山）を司る神で、嘔吐物から産まれたとしたのは、嘔吐物の外観からの連想によるものと考えられる。鉱山を司どり、また荒金を採る神とされ、[[鉱業]]・[[鍛冶]]など、[[金属]]に関する技工を守護する神とされている。[[岐阜県]][[垂井町]]の[[南宮大社]]（金山彦神のみ）、[[宮城県]][[涌谷町]]の[[黄金山神社]]を始め、全国の'''金山神社'''で祀られている。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の神の一覧]] *[[アマツマラ]] *[[イシコリドメ]]
[[Category:日本の神|かなやまひこ]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 4.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナミの病と死によって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 4.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''金山彦神'''（かなやまひこのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。『[[古事記]]』では'''金山毘古神'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''金山彦神'''と表記する。'''金山毘売神'''（かなやまびめのかみ、金山姫神）とともに[[鉱山]]の神として信仰されている。
[[神産み]]において、[[イザナミ]]が火の神[[カグツチ]]を産んで火傷をし病み苦しんでいるときに、その嘔吐物（たぐり）から化生した神である。『古事記』では金山毘古神・金山毘売神の二神、『日本書紀』の第三の一書では金山彦神のみが化生している。
神名の通り「金山」（かなやま、鉱山）を司る神で、嘔吐物から産まれたとしたのは、嘔吐物の外観からの連想によるものと考えられる。鉱山を司どり、また荒金を採る神とされ、[[鉱業]]・[[鍛冶]]など、[[金属]]に関する技工を守護する神とされている。[[島根県]][[安来市]]の[[金屋子神社]]、[[岐阜県]][[垂井町]]の[[南宮大社]]（金山彦神のみ）、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]][[金華山 (宮城県)|金華山]]の[[黄金山神社 (石巻市)|黄金山神社]]を始め、全国の'''金山神社'''で祀られている。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の神の一覧]] *[[アマツマラ]] *[[イシコリドメ]]

revision.comment: {{Izanami-firebirths}}+ [[User:Michey.M]]さんの家系図の画像[[File:Creation myths of Japan 4.svg]]を模したパレット式テンプレート
revision.text:
[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 4.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナミの病と死によって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 4.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''金山彦神'''（かなやまひこのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''金山毘古神'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''金山彦神'''と表記する。'''金山毘売神'''（かなやまびめのかみ、金山姫神）とともに[[鉱山]]の神として信仰されている。
[[神産み]]において、[[イザナミ]]が火の神[[カグツチ]]を産んで火傷をし病み苦しんでいるときに、その嘔吐物（たぐり）から化生した神である。『古事記』では金山毘古神・金山毘売神の二神、『日本書紀』の第三の一書では金山彦神のみが化生している。
神名の通り「金山」（かなやま、鉱山）を司る神で、嘔吐物から産まれたとしたのは、嘔吐物の外観からの連想によるものと考えられる。鉱山を司どり、また[[荒金]]を採る神とされ、[[鉱業]]・[[鍛冶]]など、[[金属]]に関する技工を守護する神とされている。[[岐阜県]][[垂井町]]の[[南宮大社]]（金山彦神のみ）、[[南宮御旅神社]]（金山姫神のみ）、[[島根県]][[安来市]]の[[金屋子神社]]、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]][[金華山 (宮城県)|金華山]]の[[黄金山神社 (石巻市)|黄金山神社]]を始め、全国の'''金山神社'''で祀られている。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の神の一覧]] *[[天津麻羅|アマツマラ]] *[[イシコリドメ]]
{{Izanami-firebirths}} {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かなやまひこ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:鍛冶神]]

[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 4.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナミの病と死によって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]] '''金山彦神'''（かなやまひこのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''金山毘古神'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''金山彦神'''と表記する。'''金山毘売神'''（かなやまびめのかみ、金山姫神）とともに[[鉱山]]の神として信仰されている。
[[神産み]]において、[[イザナミ]]が火の神[[カグツチ]]を産んで火傷をし病み苦しんでいるときに、その嘔吐物（たぐり）から化生した神である。『古事記』では金山毘古神・金山毘売神の二神、『日本書紀』の第三の一書では金山彦神のみが化生している。
神名の通り「金山」（かなやま、鉱山）を司る神で、嘔吐物から産まれたとしたのは、嘔吐物の外観からの連想によるものと考えられる。鉱山を司どり、また[[荒金]]を採る神とされ、[[鉱業]]・[[鍛冶]]など、[[金属]]に関する技工を守護する神とされている。[[岐阜県]][[垂井町]]の[[南宮大社]]（金山彦神のみ）、[[南宮御旅神社]]（金山姫神のみ）、[[島根県]][[安来市]]の[[金屋子神社]]、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]][[金華山 (宮城県)|金華山]]の[[黄金山神社 (石巻市)|黄金山神社]]、[[京都府]][[京都市]]の[[幡枝八幡宮]]末社の針神社を始め、全国の'''金山神社'''で祀られている。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の神の一覧]] *[[天津麻羅|アマツマラ]] *[[イシコリドメ]]

== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''金山毘古神'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''金山彦神'''と表記する。'''金山毘売神'''（かなやまびめのかみ、金山姫神）と対になるともされる。

[[岐阜県]][[垂井町]]の[[南宮大社]]（金山彦神のみ）、[[南宮御旅神社]]（金山姫神のみ）、[[島根県]][[安来市]]の[[金屋子神社]]、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]][[金華山 (宮城県)|金華山]]の[[黄金山神社 (石巻市)|黄金山神社]]、[[京都府]][[京都市]]の[[御金神社]]及び[[幡枝八幡宮]]末社の針神社を始め、全国の'''金山神社'''で祀られている。

revision.text:
[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 4.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナミの病と死によって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]] '''金山彦神'''（かなやまひこのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。

[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 4.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナミの病と死によって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]] '''金山彦神'''（かなやまひこのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''金山毘古神'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''金山彦神'''と表記する。'''金山毘売神'''（かなやまびめのかみ、金山姫神）と対偶をなす神である<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=金山毘古神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher=[[國學院大學]]}}</ref>。「金山」は鉱山と解するのが一般的で<ref name=":0" />、金山毘古神・金山毘売神の二神は鉱山の神と解釈される<ref name=":0" />。
[[神産み]]において、[[イザナミ]]が火の神[[カグツチ]]を産んで火傷をし病み苦しんでいるときに、その嘔吐物（たぐり）から化生した神である<ref name=":0" />。『古事記』では金山毘古神・金山毘売神の二神、『日本書紀』の第三の一書では金山彦神のみが化生している<ref name=":0" />。
[[岐阜県]][[垂井町]]の[[南宮大社]]（金山彦神のみ）、[[南宮御旅神社]]（金山姫神のみ）、[[島根県]][[安来市]]の[[金屋子神社]]、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]][[金華山 (宮城県)|金華山]]の[[黄金山神社 (石巻市)|黄金山神社]]、[[京都府]][[京都市]]の[[御金神社]]及び[[幡枝八幡宮]]末社の針神社を始め、全国の'''[[金山神社]]'''で祀られている。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の神の一覧]] *[[天津麻羅|アマツマラ]] *[[イシコリドメ]]

[[Image:Descendants of Izanami alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナミの病と死によって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]] '''金山彦神'''（かなやまひこのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。[[天津国玉神]]の父。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''金山毘古神'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''金山彦神'''と表記する。'''金山毘売神'''（かなやまびめのかみ、金山姫神）と対偶をなす神である<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=金山毘古神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher=[[國學院大學]]}}</ref>。「金山」は鉱山と解するのが一般的で<ref name=":0" />、金山毘古神・金山毘売神の二神は鉱山の神と解釈される<ref name=":0" />。
[[神産み]]において、[[イザナミ]]が火の神[[カグツチ]]を産んで火傷をし病み苦しんでいるときに、その嘔吐物（たぐり）から化生した神である<ref name=":0" />。『古事記』では金山毘古神・金山毘売神の二神、『日本書紀』の第三の一書では金山彦神のみが化生している<ref name=":0" />。
[[岐阜県]][[垂井町]]の[[南宮大社]]（金山彦神のみ）、[[南宮御旅神社]]（金山姫神のみ）、[[島根県]][[安来市]]の[[金屋子神社]]、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]][[金華山 (宮城県)|金華山]]の[[黄金山神社 (石巻市)|黄金山神社]]、[[京都府]][[京都市]]の[[御金神社]]及び[[幡枝八幡宮]]末社の針神社を始め、全国の'''[[金山神社]]'''で祀られている。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の神の一覧]] *[[天津麻羅|アマツマラ]] *[[イシコリドメ]]
{{Izanami-firebirths}} {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かなやまひこ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:鍛冶神]]

[[Image:Descendants of Izanami alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナミの病と死によって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]] '''金山彦神'''（かなやまひこのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。[[天津国玉神]]の父という説がある。

[[神産み]]において、[[イザナミ]]が火の神[[カグツチ]]を産んで火傷をし病み苦しんでいるときに、その嘔吐物（たぐり）から化生した神である<ref name=":0" />。『古事記』では金山毘古神・金山毘売神の二神、『日本書紀』の第三の一書では金山彦神のみが化生している<ref name=":0" />。
[[岐阜県]][[垂井町]]の[[南宮大社]]（金山彦神のみ）、[[南宮御旅神社]]（金山姫神のみ）、[[島根県]][[安来市]]の[[金屋子神社]]、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]][[金華山 (宮城県)|金華山]]の[[黄金山神社 (石巻市)|黄金山神社]]、[[京都府]][[京都市]]の[[御金神社]]及び[[幡枝八幡宮]]末社の針神社を始め、全国の'''[[金山神社]]'''で祀られている。

revision.text:
[[ja:金山彦神]] [[Image:Descendants of Izanami alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the ''Kojiki'') [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]] '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. Some theories suggest he is the father of {{ill|Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|ja|天津国玉神}}.
== Overview == In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', he is referred to as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山毘古神), while in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', he is written as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神). He forms a divine pair with {{ill|Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja|金山毘売神}} (also known as Kanayamahime-no-Kami, 金山姫神)<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Kojiki Study Center Website |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher={{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}}}</ref>. The term "Kanayama" is generally interpreted as referring to a mine<ref name=":0" />, and the deities Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are understood as gods of metal and mining<ref name=":0" />.
According to the myth of {{ill|Kamiumi|ja|神産み}} ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}} when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|ja|カグツチ}}<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.
Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including {{ill|Nangū Taisha|ja|南宮大社}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社}} in {{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)}} on {{ill|Kinkasan|ja|金華山 (宮城県)}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki, Miyagi|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Ama-Tsu-Mara|ja|天津麻羅}} * {{ill|Ishikori-dome no Mikoto|ja|イシコリドメ}}
{{Izanami-firebirths}} {{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanayamahiko}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Deities of blacksmithing]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]][[sn:Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime]][[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:金山彦神]] [[Image:Descendants of Izanami alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the ''Kojiki'') [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]] '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. Some theories suggest he is the father of {{ill|Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|ja|天津国玉神}}.
== Overview == In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', he is referred to as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山毘古神), while in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', he is written as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神). He forms a divine pair with {{ill|Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja|金山毘売神}} (also known as Kanayamahime-no-Kami, 金山姫神)<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Kojiki Study Center Website |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher={{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}}}</ref>. The term "Kanayama" is generally interpreted as referring to a mine<ref name=":0" />, and the deities Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are understood as gods of metal and mining<ref name=":0" />.
According to the myth of {{ill|Kamiumi|ja|神産み}} ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}} when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|ja|カグツチ}}<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.
Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including {{ill|Nangū Taisha|ja|南宮大社}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社}} in {{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|ja|安来市}}, {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)}} on {{ill|Kinkasan|ja|金華山 (宮城県)}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki, Miyagi|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Ama-Tsu-Mara|ja|天津麻羅}} * {{ill|Ishikori-dome no Mikoto|ja|イシコリドメ}}

revision.text:
[[Image:Descendants of Izanami alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the ''Kojiki'') [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]] '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. Some theories suggest he is the father of {{ill|Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|ja|天津国玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Overview == In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he is referred to as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山毘古神), while in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is written as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神). He forms a divine pair with {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja|金山毘売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}} (also known as Kanayamahime-no-Kami, 金山姫神)<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Kojiki Study Center Website |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher=[[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]}}</ref>. The term "Kanayama" is generally interpreted as referring to a mine<ref name=":0" />, and the deities Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are understood as gods of metal and mining<ref name=":0" />.
According to the myth of [[:en:Kamiumi|Kamiumi]] ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god [[:en:Kagu-tsuchi|Kagu-tsuchi]]<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.
Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including [[:en:Nangū Taisha|Nangū Taisha]] (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangu Otabi Shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayago Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|qq=}} on [[:en:Kinkasan|Kinkasan]] in [[:en:Ishinomaki|Ishinomaki, Miyagi]], {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikane Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}.

Some theories suggest he is the father of {{ill|Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|ja|天津国玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

He forms a divine pair with {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja|金山毘売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}} (also known as Kanayamahime-no-Kami, 金山姫神)<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Kojiki Study Center Website |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher=[[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]}}</ref>. The term "Kanayama" is generally interpreted as referring to a mine<ref name=":0" />, and the deities Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are understood as gods of metal and mining<ref name=":0" />.
According to the myth of [[:en:Kamiumi|Kamiumi]] ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god [[:en:Kagu-tsuchi|Kagu-tsuchi]]<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.
Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including [[:en:Nangū Taisha|Nangū Taisha]] (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangu Otabi Shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayago Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|qq=}} on [[:en:Kinkasan|Kinkasan]] in [[:en:Ishinomaki|Ishinomaki, Miyagi]], {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikane Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}.

{{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|deity_of=Kami of {{ill|mine|qid=Q820477}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q4387856]] Wikidata name: Kanayamahiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q4387856]] Wikidata name: Kanayamahiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanami alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the ''Kojiki'') [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]] '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. Some theories suggest he is the father of {{ill|Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|ja|天津国玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Overview == In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he is referred to as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山毘古神), while in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is written as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神). He forms a divine pair with {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja|金山毘売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}} (also known as Kanayamahime-no-Kami, 金山姫神)<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Kojiki Study Center Website |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher=[[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]}}</ref>. The term "Kanayama" is generally interpreted as referring to a mine<ref name=":0" />, and the deities Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are understood as gods of metal and mining<ref name=":0" />.
According to the myth of [[:en:Kamiumi|Kamiumi]] ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god [[:en:Kagu-tsuchi|Kagu-tsuchi]]<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.
Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including [[:en:Nangū Taisha|Nangū Taisha]] (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangu Otabi Shrine|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nangū Otabi Shrine|WD=Q11407720}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayago Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|qq=}} on [[:en:Kinkasan|Kinkasan]] in [[:en:Ishinomaki|Ishinomaki, Miyagi]], {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikane Shrine|qq=|1=Mikane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489342}} in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Hataeda-hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594565}}.
== References == <references />

Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including [[:en:Nangū Taisha|Nangū Taisha]] (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangu Otabi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nangū Otabi Shrine|WD=Q11407720}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|qq=}} on [[:en:Kinkasan|Kinkasan]] in [[:en:Ishinomaki|Ishinomaki, Miyagi]], {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikane Shrine|1=Mikane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489342}} in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|1=Hataeda-hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594565}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|deity_of=Kami of {{ill|mine|WD=Q820477}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q4387856]] Wikidata name: Kanayamahiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q4387856]] Wikidata name: Kanayamahiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanami alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the ''Kojiki'') [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]] '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. Some theories suggest he is the father of {{ill|Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|ja|天津国玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Overview == In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he is referred to as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山毘古神), while in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is written as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神). He forms a divine pair with {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja|金山毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}} (also known as Kanayamahime-no-Kami, 金山姫神)<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Kojiki Study Center Website |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher=[[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]}}</ref>. The term "Kanayama" is generally interpreted as referring to a mine<ref name=":0" />, and the deities Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are understood as gods of metal and mining<ref name=":0" />.
According to the myth of [[:en:Kamiumi|Kamiumi]] ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god [[:en:Kagu-tsuchi|Kagu-tsuchi]]<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.
Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including [[:en:Nangū Taisha|Nangū Taisha]] (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangu Otabi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nangū Otabi Shrine|WD=Q11407720}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|qq=}} on [[:en:Kinkasan|Kinkasan]] in [[:en:Ishinomaki|Ishinomaki, Miyagi]], {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikane Shrine|1=Mikane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489342}} in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|1=Hataeda-hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594565}}.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:Ama-Tsu-Mara|Ama-Tsu-Mara]] * [[:en:Ishikori-dome no Mikoto|Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]

Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including [[:en:Nangū Taisha|Nangū Taisha]] (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangu Otabi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nangū Otabi Shrine|WD=Q11407720}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|qq=}} on [[:en:Kinkasan|Kinkasan]] in [[:en:Ishinomaki|Ishinomaki, Miyagi]], {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikane Shrine|1=Mikane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489342}} in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|1=Hataeda-hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594565}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|deity_of=Kami of {{ill|mine|WD=Q820477}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q4387856]] Wikidata name: Kanayamahiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q4387856]] Wikidata name: Kanayamahiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanami alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the ''Kojiki'') [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]] '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. Some theories suggest he is the father of {{ill|Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|ja|天津国玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Overview == In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he is referred to as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山毘古神), while in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is written as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神). He forms a divine pair with {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja|金山毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}} (also known as Kanayamahime-no-Kami, 金山姫神)<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Kojiki Study Center Website |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher=[[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]}}</ref>. The term "Kanayama" is generally interpreted as referring to a mine<ref name=":0" />, and the deities Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are understood as gods of metal and mining<ref name=":0" />.
According to the myth of [[:en:Kamiumi|Kamiumi]] ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god [[:en:Kagu-tsuchi|Kagu-tsuchi]]<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.
Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including [[:en:Nangū Taisha|Nangū Taisha]] (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangu Otabi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nangū Otabi Shrine|WD=Q11407720}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|qq=}} on [[:en:Kinkasan|Kinkasan]] in [[:en:Ishinomaki|Ishinomaki, Miyagi]], {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikane Shrine|1=Mikane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489342}} in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|1=Hataeda-hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594565}}.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:Ama-Tsu-Mara|Ama-Tsu-Mara]] * [[:en:Ishikori-dome no Mikoto|Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]

He forms a divine pair with {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja|金山毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}} (also known as Kanayamahime-no-Kami, 金山姫神)<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Kojiki Study Center Website |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher=[[:en:Kokugakuin University|Kokugakuin University]]}}</ref>. According to the myth of [[:en:Kamiumi|Kamiumi]] ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god [[:en:Kagu-tsuchi|Kagu-tsuchi]]<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|deity_of=Kami of {{ill|mine|WD=Q820477}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q4387856]] Wikidata name: Kanayamahiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q4387856]] Wikidata name: Kanayamahiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanami alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the ''Kojiki'') [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]] '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=Deity}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. Some theories suggest he is the father of {{ill|Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|ja|天津国玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Overview == In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', he is referred to as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山毘古神), while in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', he is written as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神). He forms a divine pair with {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja|金山毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}} (also known as Kanayamahime-no-Kami, 金山姫神)<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Kojiki Study Center Website |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}}}}</ref>. The term "Kanayama" is generally interpreted as referring to a mine<ref name=":0" />, and the deities Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are understood as gods of metal and mining<ref name=":0" />.
According to the myth of {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Kamiumi}} ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagu-tsuchi}}<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.
Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including {{ill|Nangū Taisha|en|Nangū Taisha|WD=Q704967|lt=Nangū Taisha}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|en|Tarui, Gifu|WD=Q1345531|lt=Tarui, Gifu}}, {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangu Otabi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nangū Otabi Shrine|WD=Q11407720}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|en|Yasugi, Shimane|WD=Q851114|lt=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|qq=}} on {{ill|Kinkasan|en|Kinkasan|WD=Q6413909|lt=Kinkasan}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|WD=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikane Shrine|1=Mikane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489342}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|1=Hataeda-hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594565}}.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Ama-Tsu-Mara|en|Ama-Tsu-Mara|WD=Q10940368|lt=Ama-Tsu-Mara}} * {{ill|Ishikori-dome no Mikoto|en|Ishikori-dome no Mikoto|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}

Some theories suggest he is the father of {{ill|Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|ja|天津国玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

The term "Kanayama" is generally interpreted as referring to a mine<ref name=":0" />, and the deities Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are understood as gods of metal and mining<ref name=":0" />.
According to the myth of {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Kamiumi}} ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagu-tsuchi}}<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.
Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including {{ill|Nangū Taisha|en|Nangū Taisha|WD=Q704967|lt=Nangū Taisha}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|en|Tarui, Gifu|WD=Q1345531|lt=Tarui, Gifu}}, {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangu Otabi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nangū Otabi Shrine|WD=Q11407720}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|en|Yasugi, Shimane|WD=Q851114|lt=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|qq=}} on {{ill|Kinkasan|en|Kinkasan|WD=Q6413909|lt=Kinkasan}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|WD=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikane Shrine|1=Mikane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489342}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|1=Hataeda-hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594565}}.

'''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=Deity}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. Some theories suggest he is the father of {{ill|Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|ja|天津国玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

== Overview ==
In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', he is referred to as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山毘古神), while in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', he is written as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神). He forms a divine pair with {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja|金山毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}} (also known as Kanayamahime-no-Kami, 金山姫神)<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Kojiki Study Center Website |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}}}}</ref>. The term "Kanayama" is generally interpreted as referring to a mine<ref name=":0" />, and the deities Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are understood as gods of metal and mining<ref name=":0" />.

According to the myth of {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Kamiumi}} ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagu-tsuchi}}<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.

Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including {{ill|Nangū Taisha|en|Nangū Taisha|WD=Q704967|lt=Nangū Taisha}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|en|Tarui, Gifu|WD=Q1345531|lt=Tarui, Gifu}}, {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangu Otabi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nangū Otabi Shrine|WD=Q11407720}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|en|Yasugi, Shimane|WD=Q851114|lt=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|qq=}} on {{ill|Kinkasan|en|Kinkasan|WD=Q6413909|lt=Kinkasan}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|WD=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikane Shrine|1=Mikane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489342}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|1=Hataeda-hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594565}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[es:Kanayamahiko y Kanayamahime]][[ja:金山彦神]][[pl:Kanayamahiko]][[zh:金山毘古神]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|deity_of=Kami of {{ill|mine|WD=Q820477}}}}
[[Image:Descendants of Izanami alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the ''Kojiki'') [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0a/Creation_myths_of_Japan_4.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]] '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=Deity}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. Some theories suggest he is the father of {{ill|Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|ja|天津国玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Overview == In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', he is referred to as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山毘古神), while in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', he is written as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神). He forms a divine pair with {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja|金山毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}} (also known as Kanayamahime-no-Kami, 金山姫神)<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Kojiki Study Center Website |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University}}}}</ref>. The term "Kanayama" is generally interpreted as referring to a mine<ref name=":0" />, and the deities Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are understood as gods of metal and mining<ref name=":0" />.
According to the myth of {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Kamiumi}} ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagu-tsuchi}}<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.
Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including {{ill|Nangū Taisha|en|Nangū Taisha|WD=Q704967|lt=Nangū Taisha}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|en|Tarui, Gifu|WD=Q1345531|lt=Tarui, Gifu}}, {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangu Otabi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nangū Otabi Shrine|WD=Q11407720}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|en|Yasugi, Shimane|WD=Q851114|lt=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|qq=}} on {{ill|Kinkasan|en|Kinkasan|WD=Q6413909|lt=Kinkasan}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|WD=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikane Shrine|1=Mikane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489342}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|1=Hataeda-hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594565}}.

'''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=Deity|1=Kami}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. Some theories suggest he is the father of {{ill|Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|ja|天津国玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukunitama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

== Overview ==
In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', he is referred to as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山毘古神), while in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', he is written as '''Kanayamahiko-no-Kami''' (金山彦神). He forms a divine pair with {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja|金山毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}} (also known as Kanayamahime-no-Kami, 金山姫神)<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Kojiki Study Center Website |url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kanayamabikonokami/ |website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp |access-date=2022-10-10 |publisher={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|Kokugakuin University|WD=Q495330|lt=Kokugakuin University|1=Kokugakuin University}}}}</ref>. The term "Kanayama" is generally interpreted as referring to a mine<ref name=":0" />, and the deities Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are understood as gods of metal and mining<ref name=":0" />.

According to the myth of {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Kamiumi|1=Kamiumi}} ("divine birth"), Kanayamahiko-no-Kami was born from the vomit of {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} when she suffered burns and fell ill after giving birth to the fire god {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}<ref name=":0" />. In the ''Kojiki'', both Kanayamahiko-no-Kami and Kanayamahime-no-Kami are said to have been born this way, while in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Third Alternate Writing), only Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is mentioned<ref name=":0" />.

Kanayamahiko-no-Kami is enshrined in shrines across Japan, including {{ill|Nangū Taisha|en|Nangū Taisha|WD=Q704967|lt=Nangū Taisha|1=Nangū Taisha}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahiko-no-Kami) in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|en|Tarui, Gifu|WD=Q1345531|lt=Tarui, Gifu|1=Tarui, Gifu}}, {{ill|Nangu Otabi Shrine|ja|南宮御旅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nangu Otabi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nangū Otabi Shrine|WD=Q11407720}} (dedicated solely to Kanayamahime-no-Kami), {{ill|Kanayago Shrine|ja|金屋子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|en|Yasugi, Shimane|WD=Q851114|lt=Yasugi, Shimane|1=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|黄金山神社 (石巻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganeyama Shrine (Ishinomaki)|qq=}} on {{ill|Kinkasan|en|Kinkasan|WD=Q6413909|lt=Kinkasan|1=Kinkasan}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|WD=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki, Miyagi|1=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|Mikane Shrine|ja|御金神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikane Shrine|1=Mikane Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489342}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, and {{ill|Hari Shrine|ja|針神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|ja|幡枝八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataeda Hachiman Shrine|1=Hataeda-hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594565}}.
